1.response: I would consider this as a very vague smile, if a smile at all. It's a mixture of polite smile and a shy smile.

5.response: If I would consider this being a smile, I would call it a polite smile. But I am not sure, whether or not I would have noticed that as a smile watching the whole video.

6.response: Genuin smile due to happy memories.

10.response: Mixture between genuine smile (remembering beeing married) and self-irony (because getting married within to weeks)

13.response: This is such a case in which I would say, I would need to see more of the interview in order to be able to judge whether this is a smile or not. If one looks at the wrinkles at her eyes, the lady looks like she had smiled a lot during her live. Maybe that's just her normal expression in this sequence.

14.response: Genuin smile mixed with a little bit of irony

15.response: In the yellow marked sequence I do not see a smile. But shorly beforhand, there is a very small, very sad smile - which, nevertheless, is a genuine smile - a genuin sad smile I'd call it.

18.response: Genuin smile on the memory of realizing that the Nazis were gone

20.response: Genuin smile, mixture of pride and self-irony, because it is about learing fast to adept to an evil environment.

25.response: Genuin smile, sad and and a bit self-ironic

29.response: Genuin smile with a hough heartly felt portion of humor and self-irony!

33.response: Interaktive smile. I'd not describe it as a polite smile, but it has something to do with the social situation, with telling someone a story.

34.response: Genuin smile on the memory of a teacher he liked.

37.response: Genuin smile while talking about his family

39.response: A smile that is mocking the SS.

40.response: Genuin smile on childhood memories about shabbes.

43.response: In the yellow section is no smile, but shortly after there is a genuin smile on a happy memory.

44.response: It's a genuin smile. I contains self-irony, but it also has the quality of a mocking smile making fun of the Germans.

45.response: The smile contains elements of shame, self-irony. It's kind of a masking smile that shields her and the interviewer from the horrible reality that she is describing.

47.response: Genuin smile that contains on one hand self-irony (there is a bomb exploding and he makes butter) on the other hand it contains relief, mabye even happyness,  about having survied.

48.response: Well, if the attention is drawn to it, one might notice a very vague smile that I cannot really classify. But I am pretty sure that I would not have noticed that as a smile myself when I would watch the whole interview.

50.response: narrative smile that masks sad feelings in the past - the Germans did not bother to say sorry and that hurts

53.response: Genuin smile, because he is about to tell a story that he finds funny, even though it is based in the hardships of the past experience

54.response: Genuin smile on a happy and funny childhood memory

55.response: Genuin smile on a happy childhood memory.

56.response: Genuin smile remembering his parent's strategy to avoid children listing to things they were not supposed to hear by talking in Yiddish or Russian

57.response: Genuin smile thinking about her children.

59.response: Genuin smile that contains mixed feelings: happy memories, becaus it is about playing a game, but at the same time sadness, because it was a game under very poor condition. Even the joy of playing could not hide the difficult circumstances of life back then.

60.response: Genuin smile remembering his grandmother, contains pride about her beeing an impressive and educated woman. It's a smile that I did not understand, before I read a fair bit of the transcript.

61.response: Genuin, but very vague smile on a happy memory, contains maybe a bit of regret or self-irony, because he had to get engaged secretly.

63.response: Not a smile. There is a vague, smile-like facial movement, though, which has, from my point of view, a social function - something like keeping-up the social bond with the interviewer

64.response: Genuin smile on a happy memory.

65.response: Genuin smile. Even tough he is talking about the horrible conditions in the deportation trains, he does it in a humorous way. His smile and laugh contain self-irony, but have as well a social function, since they are shielding himself and the interviewer from the dreatful experience that lays behind his narrative. Nevertheless, the smile and laughing, to me, seem heartfelt, thats why I would not reduce them to merely social function or a masking smile. To me, they are expressions of the ability to emotionally deal with negative experiences by using humour.

66.response: Social smile for reassuring the listener's attention.

67.response: Genuin smile on the memory of a moment of happyness and relief seeing a friend.

70.response: Genuin smile remembering her mother and how she protected them.

72.response: Genuin smile, contains pride and irony, because he is telling how he outwitted Guards

73.response: Genuin smile remembering how he managed life in France, contains pride of doing it well. At the same time it seems to have a social function: engaging with the interviewer and reassuring attention.

74.response: Masking smile, shields her and the listeners from negative feelings in the past: feeling ashamed in front of a whole class.

76.response: Polite smile that masks the own unabilty (or unwillingsness) to answer the interviewer's question.

79.response: The yellow section does not contain a smile. Shortly afterwards there is a social smile. It contains shyness, nervousness.

80.response: Social smile, contains pride on the own knowledge, establishes a hierachiy of knowledge between interviewee and interviewer

81.response: Genuin smile on a funny memory (becoming political which seemed to be something like and act of friedly rebellion against her mother's whishes)

84.response: Genuin smile describing a school building that he find impressive and that he - judged from his facial expression - liked.

85.response: Genuin smile on happy childhood memories

86.response: Social smile. It's meaning it really hard to tackle: mocking her former employers, who never made use of her language skills, reassuring the interviewer's attention follows her interpretation of the situation she is describing, maybe it also masks past dissapointment

87.response: Smile contains a genuin component (memory of a guy who helped him saving his valuables) and a social component (to keep the listener engaged)

88.response: Difficult... Cynical or ironic smile, I'd say

89.response: Genuin smile, because she tells a joke

90.response: Social smile that camouflages that she cannot answer the question about their sibling age. Reassures the interviewer's agreement into her interpretation of the situation ("we did not celebrate birthdays - maybe because of my orthodox grandmother").

93.response: Social smile that shields from sad memories

94.response: Social smile, contains cynism, tries to reassure agreement into the her interpretation of the situation

95.response: Social smile, shields herself and the interviewer from the negative emotions of her narrative

97.response: Genuin smile on remembering how the woman, she is talking about, was optimistic that her children will survive
